name: ci-cd-and-automation

description_normalized: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ci-cd-and-automation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.

name: deprecation-and-migration
description_raw: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.
description_normalized: Manages safe removal of old systems and migration of users from deprecated to new implementations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: deprecation-and-migration
frontmatter_yaml.description: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.

name: git-workflow-and-versioning
description_raw: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.
description_normalized: Enforces disciplined git workflow (trunk-based, atomic commits, safe branching) as the safety net for AI-accelerated development.

frontmatter_yaml.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.

name: shipping-and-launch
description_raw: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.
description_normalized: Runs a pre-launch checklist ensuring any production deployment is reversible, observable, and incremental with a rollback plan.

frontmatter_yaml.name: shipping-and-launch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.

name: ship
description_raw: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision
description_normalized: Fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer in parallel then merges reports into a go/no-go shipping decision.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision

name: delivery-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches review-blind-hunter, review-edge-case-hunter, review-acceptance-auditor, review-architecture, review-code-quality, and review-unit-tests as background agents, then triages and synthesizes findings into a unified verdict. Use when: 'delivery review', 'review this PR', 'review my changes', 'code review all dimensions', 'full review', 'review before merge', 'is this ready to ship'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: near-compaction
description_raw: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up. Use when user says "near compaction", "running out of context", "context is filling up", "save everything before we compact", "pre-compaction save", "almost at limit", "context nearly full", or when you notice context warnings. Runs a triage checklist and calls leaf skills in the right order — session-checkpoint, capture-context, knowledge-capture — so nothing important is lost before compaction fires.
description_normalized: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up.

name: review-acceptance-auditor
description_raw: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'. Maps ACs to code evidence. Use when: 'check ACs', 'acceptance audit', 'does this meet the spec', 'verify acceptance criteria', 'AC coverage'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AA dimension).
description_normalized: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'.

name: review-architecture
description_raw: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions. Use when: 'architecture review', 'does this follow the architecture', 'check patterns', 'convention compliance', 'structural alignment'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AR dimension).
description_normalized: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions.

name: review-blind-hunter
description_raw: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight. Cynical, skeptical analysis of code changes — looks for what feels off even when it technically works. Use when: 'blind hunt', 'adversarial review', 'what am I missing', 'find hidden issues', 'cynical review'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (BH dimension).
description_normalized: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight.

name: review-code-quality
description_raw: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code. Fast, diff-scoped quality check — not a full audit. Use when: 'code quality review', 'is this clean', 'quality check', 'naming review', 'DRY check'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (CQ dimension).
description_normalized: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code.

name: review-edge-case-hunter
description_raw: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases. Method-driven path enumeration, not intuition. Use when: 'edge cases', 'boundary conditions', 'what could break', 'missing validations', 'path analysis'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (EC dimension).
description_normalized: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases.

name: review-unit-tests
description_raw: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code. Reviews test design, not test execution. Use when: 'test review', 'are tests sufficient', 'test coverage check', 'missing tests', 'test quality'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (UT dimension).
description_normalized: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code.

name: system-audit
description_raw: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report. Unlike delivery-review (which is diff-scoped and story-focused), system-audit reads the full codebase and produces a severity-ranked health map. Use when user says: "system audit", "full audit", "codebase health check", "audit the whole system", "audit this codebase", "run a health check", "full system review", "review the whole codebase", "audit before onboarding", "what's wrong with this codebase". Supported stacks: Next.js App Router, RVETS (AppyStack), Baku (claude.ai/claude-ship), Astro, Rails, Ruby gem, AppySentinel. Accepts a manual layer manifest for any other stack.
description_normalized: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report.

name: progress
description_raw: Progress tracking for FliVideo project — track and report development progress.
description_normalized: Track and report development progress for the FliVideo project.

name: ce-clean-gone-branches
description_raw: Clean up local branches whose remote tracking branch is gone. Use when the user says "clean up branches", "delete gone branches", "prune local branches", "clean gone", or wants to remove stale local branches that no longer exist on the remote. Also handles removing associated worktrees for branches that have them.
description_normalized: Deletes local branches whose remote tracking branch has been removed, including associated worktrees.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-clean-gone-branches
frontmatter_yaml.description: Clean up local branches whose remote tracking branch is gone.

name: ce-commit-push-pr
description_raw: Commit, push, and open a PR with an adaptive, value-first description that scales in depth with the change. Use when the user says "commit and PR", "ship this", "create a PR", or "open a pull request". Also handles description-only flows without committing or pushing.
description_normalized: Commits, pushes, and opens a PR with an adaptive value-first description; also handles description-only and description-update flows.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-commit-push-pr
frontmatter_yaml.description: Commit, push, and open a PR with an adaptive, value-first description that scales in depth with the change.

name: ce-commit
description_raw: Create a git commit with a clear, value-communicating message. Use when the user says "commit", "commit this", "save my changes", "create a commit", or wants to commit staged or unstaged work. Produces well-structured commit messages that follow repo conventions when they exist, and defaults to conventional commit format otherwise.
description_normalized: Creates a well-structured git commit message that follows repo conventions or conventional commit format as a fallback.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-commit
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create a git commit with a clear, value-communicating message.

name: ce-demo-reel
description_raw: Capture a visual demo reel (GIF, terminal recording, screenshots) for PR descriptions. Use when shipping UI changes, CLI features, or any work with observable behavior that benefits from visual proof. Also use when asked to add a demo, record a GIF, screenshot a feature, show what changed visually, create a demo reel, capture evidence, add proof to a PR, or create a before/after comparison.
description_normalized: Detects project type, records visual evidence of product usage, uploads to a public URL, and returns markdown for PR inclusion.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-demo-reel
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture a visual demo reel (GIF, terminal recording, screenshots) for PR descriptions.

name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
description_raw: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel. Use when addressing PR review comments, resolving review threads, or fixing code review feedback.
description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, spawns parallel resolver agents per thread, implements fixes, replies, and resolves threads.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
frontmatter_yaml.description: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [PR number, comment URL, or blank for current branch's PR]

name: ce-worktree
description_raw: Create an isolated git worktree for parallel feature work or PR review. Use when starting work that should not disturb the current checkout, or when ce-work or ce-code-review offers a worktree option.
description_normalized: Creates an isolated git worktree under .worktrees/<branch> with env file copying, mise/direnv trust, and gitignore setup.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-worktree
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create an isolated git worktree for parallel feature work or PR review.

name: lfg
description_raw: Run the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end (plan, work, code review, test, commit, push, open PR, watch CI, fix CI failures until green). Use only when the user explicitly requests hands-off execution of a software task and provides a feature description; do not auto-route casual conversation here.
description_normalized: Runs the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end: plan → work → code review → commit → push → PR → CI watch, gated at each step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: lfg
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end.

description_normalized: Produces executable Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and post-deploy monitoring plans for risky data PRs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-deployment-verification-agent
frontmatter_yaml.description: Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists for risky data deployments.

name: ce-pr-comment-resolver
description_raw: Evaluates and resolves one or more related PR review threads -- assesses validity, implements fixes, and returns structured summaries with reply text. Spawned by the resolve-pr-feedback skill.
description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, implements fixes, and returns structured summaries with reply text; supports standard and cluster modes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-pr-comment-resolver
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates and resolves PR review threads.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit

name: gsd:pr-branch
description_raw: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out .planning/ commits — ready for code review. Solves the problem of PR diffs being cluttered with PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, STATE.md artifacts.
description_normalized: Creates a clean PR branch by cherry-picking commits that exclude .planning/ artifacts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:pr-branch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out .planning/ commits — ready for code review

name: gsd:progress
description_raw: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan). Provides situational awareness before continuing work.
description_normalized: Shows project progress summary and routes to the next action (execute existing plan or create a new one).

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:progress
frontmatter_yaml.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan)

name: gsd:quick
description_raw: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents. Flags: --full (enable all agents), --validate (add verification), --discuss (enable discussion), --research (enable research).
description_normalized: Executes a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) while skipping optional agent overhead.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:quick
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents

name: gsd:review-backlog
description_raw: Review and promote backlog items to active milestone. Reviews all 999.x backlog items and optionally promotes them into the active milestone sequence or removes stale entries.
description_normalized: Reviews all 999.x backlog items and promotes selected ones into the active milestone or removes stale entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:review-backlog
frontmatter_yaml.description: Review and promote backlog items to active milestone

name: gsd:secure-phase
description_raw: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase. Spawns gsd-security-auditor to verify that threat mitigations declared in PLAN.md are present in implemented code.
description_normalized: Retroactively verifies that phase threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in the implemented codebase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:secure-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase

name: gsd:ship
description_raw: Create PR, run review, and prepare for merge after verification passes. Push branch, create PR with auto-generated body, optionally trigger review, and track the merge.
description_normalized: Bridges local completion to merged PR by pushing, creating a PR with auto-generated body, and tracking the merge.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:ship
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create PR, run review, and prepare for merge after verification passes

name: gsd:stats
description_raw: Display project statistics — phases, plans, requirements, git metrics, and timeline. Comprehensive project statistics including phase progress, plan execution metrics, requirements completion, git history stats.
description_normalized: Displays comprehensive project statistics: phases, plans, requirements, git metrics, and timeline.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:stats
frontmatter_yaml.description: Display project statistics — phases, plans, requirements, git metrics, and timeline

name: gsd-security-auditor
description_raw: Verifies threat mitigations from PLAN.md threat model exist in implemented code. Produces SECURITY.md. Spawned by /gsd-secure-phase. Does NOT scan blindly for new vulnerabilities — only verifies declared dispositions.
description_normalized: Verifies that declared threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in implemented code; does not scan for new vulnerabilities.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies threat mitigations from PLAN.md threat model exist in implemented code. Produces SECURITY.md. Spawned by /gsd-secure-phase.

name: canary
description_raw:
Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy". (gstack)
description_normalized: Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,

name: land-and-deploy
description_raw:
Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,
verifies production health via canary checks. Takes over after /ship
creates the PR. Use when: "merge", "land", "deploy", "merge and verify",
"land it", "ship it to production". (gstack)
description_normalized: Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,

name: landing-report
description_raw:
Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. Shows which VERSION slots
are currently claimed by open PRs, which sibling Conductor workspaces have
WIP work likely to ship soon, and what slot /ship would pick next. No
mutations — just a snapshot. Use when asked to "landing report", "what's in
the queue", "show me open PRs", or "which version do I claim next". (gstack)
description_normalized: Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. Shows which VERSION slots

name: ship
description_raw:
Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,
update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. Use when asked to "ship", "deploy",
"push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push", or "get it deployed".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT push/PR directly) when the user says code
is ready, asks about deploying, wants to push code up, or asks to create a PR. (gstack)
description_normalized: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,

description_raw: Agent skill for github-modes - invoke with $agent-github-modes
description_normalized: Agent skill for github-modes - invoke with $agent-github-modes.

description_normalized: Agent skill for pr-manager - invoke with $agent-pr-manager.

description_normalized: Agent skill for release-manager - invoke with $agent-release-manager.

description_normalized: Agent skill for release-swarm - invoke with $agent-release-swarm.

name: github-automation
description_raw: GitHub workflow automation, PR management, issue tracking, and code review coordination. Integrates with GitHub Actions and repository management. Use when: PR creation, code review, issue management, release automation, workflow setup. Skip when: local-only changes, non-GitHub repositories.
description_normalized: GitHub workflow automation, PR management, issue tracking, and code review coordination.

name: github-code-review
description_raw: Comprehensive GitHub code review with AI-powered swarm coordination
description_normalized: Comprehensive GitHub code review with AI-powered swarm coordination.

name: github-multi-repo
description_raw: Multi-repository coordination, synchronization, and architecture management with AI swarm orchestration
description_normalized: Multi-repository coordination, synchronization, and architecture management with AI swarm orchestration.

name: github-project-management
description_raw: Comprehensive GitHub project management with swarm-coordinated issue tracking, project board automation, and sprint planning
description_normalized: Comprehensive GitHub project management with swarm-coordinated issue tracking, project board automation, and sprint planning.

name: github-release-management
description_raw: Comprehensive GitHub release orchestration with AI swarm coordination for automated versioning, testing, deployment, and rollback management
description_normalized: Comprehensive GitHub release orchestration with AI swarm coordination for automated versioning, testing, deployment, and rollback management.

name: github-workflow-automation
description_raw: Advanced GitHub Actions workflow automation with AI swarm coordination, intelligent CI/CD pipelines, and comprehensive repository management
description_normalized: Advanced GitHub Actions workflow automation with AI swarm coordination, intelligent CI/CD pipelines, and comprehensive repository management.

name: witness-curator
description_raw: Maintains the cryptographically-signed witness manifest. Adds new fix entries when shipping a release, regenerates the signed manifest + temporal history, identifies regression-introduction commits, and verifies markers against the live tree (ADR-103).
description_normalized: Maintains the cryptographically-signed witness manifest.

name: taskstoissues
description_raw: Convert existing tasks into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub Issues for the feature based on available design artifacts.
description_normalized: Converts tasks.md entries into GitHub Issues via MCP, preserving dependency ordering.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Convert existing tasks into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub issues for the feature based on available design artifacts.

name: speckit.git.commit
description_raw: Auto-commit changes after a Spec Kit command completes
description_normalized: Git extension command that auto-stages and commits all changes after any Spec Kit phase completes.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Auto-commit changes after a Spec Kit command completes

name: speckit.git.remote
description_raw: Detect Git remote URL for GitHub integration
description_normalized: Git extension command that detects the remote URL for GitHub MCP integration during taskstoissues.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Detect Git remote URL for GitHub integration

name: finishing-a-development-branch
description_raw: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup. Verifies tests, detects environment (worktree vs normal repo, detached HEAD), presents exactly 4 or 3 structured options, executes choice, cleans up workspace with provenance check.
description_normalized: Branch completion skill that verifies tests and presents structured merge/PR/keep/discard options with safe cleanup.

frontmatter_yaml.name: finishing-a-development-branch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

name: verification-before-completion
description_raw: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always. Iron Law: NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE. Gate function: identify command, run it fresh, read full output, verify, then claim. Includes Rationalization Prevention table.
description_normalized: Verification gate skill that requires fresh command evidence before any completion claim, commit, or PR creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: verification-before-completion
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
